out_of_scope_note: Clinical psychiatry / suicide-prevention epidemiology in 94 adults using 42-day ecological momentary assessment of self-predicted vs experienced suicidal thoughts. Although the paper frames self-prediction as prospection and computes descriptive prediction errors (experienced minus predicted severity), it does not engage a theoretical computational framework (no predictive-coding, RL, Bayesian-observer, or rate-distortion claim) and contains no neural recording or circuit-level mechanism. Best-fitting models are statistical regressions on prior ratings, not a normative model of brain function. Falls under the prompt's out-of-scope category 'Clinical case reports / clinical epidemiology with no neural-computation content.'
